rulings.Case Title: KARIN MENG, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE OF THE ET AL VS. LLOYD T. WEST ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants Lloyd T. West And Charles E. Cobbins First Set Of Form Interrogatories And First Set Of Requests For Production Of Documents, And Motion To Deem Facts Admitted, And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 1, 2023 line 1. DEFENDANT LLOYD WEST, CHARLES COBBINS NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANTS LLOYD T. WEST AND CHARLES E. COBBINS FIRST SET OF FORM INTERROGATORIES AND FIRST SET OF REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, AND MOTION TO DEEM FACTS ADMITTED, AND MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES is continued to December 22, 2023, in Department 501, at 9:30 a.m. Report by JPT received at 5:20 p.m. on November 29, 2023 =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: KARIN MENG, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE OF THE ET AL VS. LLOYD T. WEST ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 1, 2023 line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT CHRISTIANE GIGAS, SOTHEBY'S INTERNATIONAL REALTY, A FOREIGN DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with final leave to amend for Cross-Complainant to clearly allege a contractual provision that was breached by failure "to provide diligent exercise of reasonable skill and care in the performance of the Broker's duties in properly counseling Sellers relating to the disclosure of the history of water intrusion at the Property." =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: KARIN MENG, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE OF THE ET AL VS. LLOYD T. WEST ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 1, 2023 line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT CHRISTIANE GIGAS, SOTHEBY'S INTERNATIONAL REALTY, A FOREIGN MOTION TO STRIKE Amended CROSS COMPLAINT is GRANTED with final leave to amend to allege factual basis for recovery of attorney's fees on any of the causes of action alleged in the cross-complaint. Cross-Complainant does not allege in the operative Cross-Complaint does not explain in opposition how the contractual provision for recovery of attorney's fees "in an action to enforce the compensation provisions" is applicable to this cross-complaint. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: 200 PAUL, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. UNITEDLAYER, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Amended Motion And Amended Motion To File Under Seal Portions Of Unitedlayer, Llc'S Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 1, 2023 line 2. CROSS COMPLAINANT UNITEDLAYER, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY NOTICE OF AMENDED MOTION AND AMENDED MOTION TO FILE UNDER SEAL PORTIONS OF UNITEDLAYER, LLC'S CROSS-COMPLAINT is DENIED without prejudice. Declaration in support of the motion is conclusory and does not support the request as to all portions of the complaint sought to be sealed. Cross-Complainant may re-file this motion with competent evidence addressing each item listed in the notice of motion. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: POTRERO LAUNCH AFFORDABLE, LP VS. SAVOKIA SPIGNOR ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 1, 2023 line 4. DEFENDANT SAVOKIA SPIGNOR DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Plaintiff failed to provide proper notice of Rent Board Form 1007. (SF Rent Ordinance, 37.9(c) ["A landlord shall attach a copy of the form that is in the primary language of the tenant to a notice to vacate before serving the notice . . . ."].) Plaintiff provided an outdated form. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: VALSTOCK VENTURES, LLC VS. BRIAN BROWN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery, To Establish Admissions From Defendant, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Brian Brown
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 1, 2023 line 5. PLAINTIFF VALSTOCK VENTURES, LLC NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY, TO ESTABLISH ADMISSIONS FROM DEFENDANT, AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT BRIAN BROWN is GRANTED, subject to opposition. Defendant shall provide complete verified responses without objections and produce responsive documents within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Plaintiff's Request for Admissions (Set One) is deemed admitted. Sanctions granted in the amount of $1,410. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
